title: ProvisionalArtifactId
description: Contract for cross-device resume and provisional artifact ID mapping. When a device creates an artifact before it has a canonical, server-assigned ID (e.g. during offline operation), it issues a ProvisionalArtifactId. Once connectivity is restored, the provisional ID is resolved to a canonical ID and all references are migrated. This enables local-first operation without loss of traceability.

provisionalId.description: Locally-generated provisional artifact identifier (ULID or UUID).

canonicalId.description: Server-assigned canonical artifact ID. Present only when resolutionState=resolved.

deviceId.description: Device that created the provisional ID.

artifactKind.description: Kind of artifact this ID refers to (e.g. terminal-session, web-capture, agent-execution-output).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the provisional ID was issued.

resolvedAt.description: Timestamp when the provisional ID was resolved to a canonical ID.

resolutionState.description: Current resolution state. 'conflict' means two devices issued provisional IDs for the same artifact and the conflict must be resolved. 'abandoned' means the artifact was discarded without resolution.

resumeContext.description: Cross-device resume context. Allows a different device to continue an operation started with this provisional artifact.

resumableBy.description: Device IDs permitted to resume this operation.

resumeToken.description: Opaque resume token for the originating operation.
